career_research_cornell.title: Career Research Cornell Notes
career_research_cornell.subtitle: Record useful information before judging a pathway
career_research_cornell.learning_target: I can collect career information and summarize what matters most to me.
career_research_cornell.directions: Use the cue column for categories and questions. Use the notes column for facts, examples, and details from a profile, website, video, or guest speaker.
career_research_cornell.summary_prompt: Would I want to learn more about this pathway? Why?
career_research_cornell.reflection_prompt: What is one question I still need answered?

guest_speaker_guided_notes.title: Guest Speaker Guided Notes
guest_speaker_guided_notes.subtitle: Listen for practical details about work and pathways
guest_speaker_guided_notes.learning_target: I can listen for useful career information and connect it to my own strengths or questions.
guest_speaker_guided_notes.directions: Use each box during the presentation or video profile. Write short notes, not full paragraphs.
prompts: What is this person's work or pathway?
What connects to my strengths or interests?
success_criteria: I wrote at least one useful question.
I connected the pathway to myself in an honest way.

pathway_comparison_notes.title: Pathway Comparison Notes
pathway_comparison_notes.subtitle: Compare what I learned with why it matters to me
pathway_comparison_notes.learning_target: I can compare pathway information and explain what feels relevant to me.
pathway_comparison_notes.directions: Use the left column for career facts or observations. Use the right column for your reaction, connection, or question.
pathway_comparison_notes.summary_prompt: Which pathway seems most worth exploring next?
